problem.title: Path Sum II
problem.description: Given the root of a binary tree and an integer targetSum, return all root-to-leaf paths where the sum of the node values in the path equals targetSum.

example.explanation: Return all root-to-leaf paths that sum to target. Backtracking DFS.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – DFS with Backtracking
solutions.description: Perform a depth-first traversal, appending each node's value to the current path and subtracting it from the remaining target sum. When a leaf is reached with a remaining sum of zero, a copy of the current path is recorded. Backtracking (removing the last element) restores state before exploring sibling branches.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional, List

# class TreeNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.left = left
#         self.right = right

def pathSum(root: Optional[object], targetSum: int) -> List[List[int]]:
    result: List[List[int]] = []

    def dfs(node: Optional[object], remaining: int, path: List[int]) -> None:
        if not node:
            return
        path.append(node.val)
        if not node.left and not node.right and remaining == node.val:
            result.append(list(path))
        dfs(node.left, remaining - node.val, path)
        dfs(node.right, remaining - node.val, path)
        path.pop()

    dfs(root, targetSum, [])
    return result
